source_note: Directional 2026 3PL market ranges from public fulfillment pricing guides and competitor pricing pages. Use as sales guidance, not a binding quote.

dtc_base_per_order.note: Common first-pick/order pricing often sits in the low-to-mid dollar range.

dtc_additional_item.note: Additional-item pick fees are commonly quoted below the first-pick fee.

receiving_per_pallet.note: Standard pallet receiving is often market-priced below custom inbound work.

receiving_precounted_box.note: Carton/box receiving varies heavily by count quality and ASN accuracy.

receiving_count_per_item.note: Per-piece inbound counting is usually a small variable fee.

storage_short_per_pallet_mo.note: Pallet storage varies by geography, volume, and storage complexity.

storage_cubic_foot_mo.note: Cubic-foot storage is usually used when pallet pricing would be distorted.

kitting_per_unit.note: Simple kitting can be inexpensive; custom kits should price higher.

labeling_per_unit.note: Labeling depends on label source, print needs, and error tolerance.

bagging_labeling_per_unit.note: Bagging plus labeling should not be priced below simple labeling.

wholesale_per_unit.note: Wholesale unit pricing depends on case/pallet handling assumptions.

pallet_order_per_pallet.note: Pallet-order handling is usually quoted as a minimum or per-pallet fee.

returns_per_unit.note: Returns vary based on inspection, restock, disposition, and custom steps.

monthly_tech_fee.note: Tech/account platform fees are commonly waived or bundled to close larger accounts.

customer_service_monthly.note: Customer service should be charged when fulfillment owns relationship work.

special_projects_per_hour.note: Hourly warehouse project work commonly tracks labor complexity.

integration_setup_fee.note: Setup fees are often waived, reduced, or raised based on integration complexity.

monthly_minimum.note: Minimums protect low-volume accounts and can be delayed as a concession.
